title: Retinal Vascular Permeability Protocol
author: David A. Antonetti
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yagfsbw

steps.instruction: Weigh animal and record body weight for anesthetic and dye injections
Anesthetize animal with ketamine/xylazine mixture
Make an incision on skin inside of the hind leg and carefully tear away the membranes to isolate the femoral vein
Inject FITC-BSA into the femoral vein at 2 µl/g body weight (equal to 200 mg/kg body weight) using a 31-gauge 0.3 c.c. insulin syringe (vortex FITC-BSA before use)
Apply pressure on the injection site with a sterile gauze or cotton swab to stop bleeding
Staple the incision site and allow FITC-BSA to circulate for 2 hours
Anesthetize the animal again with ketamine/xylazine
Open the abdomen and draw 0.3 ml blood from the vena cava with a 27-gauge 1 c.c. syringe
Remove needle and expel blood into a heparinized micro-cuvette
Mix blood sample by gently reversing the tube several times and keep on ice
Open the chest cavity, cannulate the heart with a blunt end 22-gauge needle into the left ventricle and incise right atrium to release pressure
Perfuse with saline (warmed to 37°C) at 20 ml/min via the left ventricle for 2 minutes
Harvest the retina and place in a pre-weighed microcentrifuge tube. Rinse the harvest tools between samples to avoid cross contamination
Centrifuge the blood sample at 2,000 x g at 20°C for 15 minutes to separate plasma
Transfer the plasma to a new microcentrifuge tube and store at -80°C
Dry the retina samples with a Speed-Vac overnight
Weigh the microcentrifuge tube containing the dry retina to obtain the dry retina weight
Add 100 µl of 1% Triton-PBS to each retina and shake overnight to extract FITC-BSA
Vortex briefly, centrifuge the microcentrifuge tubes at 17,000 x g for 30 minutes and transfer supernatant to a new microcentrifuge tube
Dilute the plasma samples with 1% Triton-PBS
Dilute the stock FITC-BSA (100 mg/ml) with 1% Triton-PBS and make serial dilutions to obtain the FITC-BSA standards

Measure the standards (0.156 to 10 µg/ml), retina extract and diluted (1:900) plasma samples (20 µl/well, triplicate) in a 384-well black/clear bottom plate with a fluorescent plate reader (excitation 488 nm, emission 520 nm)

Calculate the FITC-BSA concentration of each sample with the standard curve NOTE: The auto-fluorescence background of the retina from animal without FITC-BSA injection should be subtracted for the permeability calculation.Additional tip that improves efficiency.
